stories.title: News24 | JSE hits a 10% correction as Iran war hurts emerging markets
stories.summary: The JSE has taken a hit, dropping 10% as the ongoing war in Iran shakes up emerging markets. This downturn could have serious implications for South Africa's economy and investments.

stories.title: ELECTRICITY CRISIS: Nelson Mandela Bay councillors walk out of critical electricity meeting amid widespread blackouts
stories.description: Nelson Mandela Bay’s electricity committee collapsed after ANC councillors walked out when the department head left, amid ongoing citywide power outages.
stories.summary: In Nelson Mandela Bay, a critical electricity meeting fell apart when ANC councillors walked out amid ongoing blackouts. This highlights the ongoing electricity crisis affecting many South Africans.

stories.title: One government decision that could end in disaster for South Africa
stories.description: An energy economist warned that years of government inaction have left the country dangerously exposed to rising prices and potential shortages.
stories.summary: An energy economist warns that South Africa's government inaction could lead to rising energy prices and shortages. This is a serious concern for all South Africans as loadshedding continues to disrupt daily life.

stories.title: News24 | WATCH | SANDF continues Gauteng crackdown for third day, targeting illegal mining
stories.summary: The SANDF is cracking down on illegal mining in Gauteng, continuing their operations for the third day. This ongoing effort aims to tackle crime and illegal activities that affect many communities.

stories.title: SA Post Office on the verge of collapse
stories.description: The South African Post Office's business rescue practitioners (BRPs) intend to liquidate the state-owned company.
stories.summary: The South African Post Office is on the verge of collapse, with plans for liquidation underway. This could impact postal services for millions of South Africans who rely on them.

stories.title: South Africa literally collapsing in front of everyone’s eyes
stories.description: Incidents of collapse are increasing across South Africa and points to serious failures in the regulated construction industry.
stories.summary: Reports indicate that incidents of collapse are increasing across South Africa, pointing to failures in the construction industry. This raises concerns about safety and infrastructure for all citizens.

stories.title: News24 | The Courier Guy, DHL add surcharge to air parcels as Iran war triggers fuel hikes
stories.summary: Fuel hikes due to the Iran war are prompting courier companies like The Courier Guy and DHL to add surcharges to air parcels. This could affect online shopping and delivery costs for many South Africans.

stories.title: HEALTH INEQUITY: Red Cross Children’s Hospital doctor using donated ‘miracle’ cystic fibrosis drug to save lives
stories.description: Trikafta, a ‘miracle’ cystic fibrosis drug, has the potential to radically improve lung function and life expectancy for patients living with the condition. However, it remains inaccessible for patients in South Africa’s public health system, pushing doctors like paediatric pulmonologist Marco Zampo
stories.summary: A doctor at Red Cross Children’s Hospital is using a new cystic fibrosis drug that could significantly improve patients' lives. This breakthrough offers hope to families affected by this condition.

stories.title: Kgosientsho Ramokgopa under fire
stories.description: The Department of Electricity and Energy says that South Africa is waiting on its latest power strategy due to disagreements with Spain.
stories.summary: Kgosientsho Ramokgopa is under fire as South Africa awaits a new power strategy amid ongoing energy challenges. This situation is crucial as the country grapples with frequent loadshedding.

stories.title: Netflix show made in South Africa hits number 1 worldwide — renewed for another season
stories.description: The second season of the live-action One Piece series has proven popular among Netflix audiences globally.
stories.summary: A South African Netflix show has hit number one worldwide and is set for another season. This success highlights the growing influence of local content on global platforms.
